rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical addition of Meldrum's acid-derived C-radicals to 1,3-dienes (e.g., 2,4-dimethyl-1,3-pentadiene) to give allylic methoxy-functionalized adducts
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Meldrum's acid (1A) generates a C-centered radical that adds to the 1,3-diene; the resulting allyl radical is further oxidized to an allyl cation which is trapped by MeOH

key_intermediates.name: Meldrum's acid C-centered radical (after anodic SET/deprotonation of 1A)
BHT-trapped Meldrum's acid radical adduct 9 (C22H32O5, cyclohexadienone-type)
Allyl radical from radical addition to diene 2a
Allyl cation (post second SET) trapped by MeOH

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Meldrum's acid 1A to a C-centered radical (via SET/deprotonation); this radical adds to the 1,3-diene to give an allyl radical, which undergoes a second anodic oxidation to an allyl cation that is trapped by methanol to deliver the methoxylated adduct 3Aa.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: With diene + BHT: 3Aa formation completely inhibited (0% yield); BHT-Meldrum's acid radical adduct 9 detected by HRMS (m/z 376.2265, calc 376.2250 for C22H32O5). Without diene + BHT under electrolysis: adduct 9 also detected by HRMS. Without electricity (BHT, 5h30, no current): adduct 9 NOT detected, confirming current is required to generate the radical.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited in presence of TEMPO; product 3Aa not obtained

notes: Control experiment without electricity but with electrodes immersed showed no BHT adduct, demonstrating that radical generation is electrochemically driven (not from background chemistry). The BHT adduct 9 is a cyclohexadienone arising from radical addition of the Meldrum's acid radical to BHT's aromatic ring.
